The man, identified as Jaber Shabana, was approached by four settlers riding an all-terrain vehicle. According to him, three of the assailants got off and physically assaulted him, knocking him to the ground. The assailants then attempted to stab him in the chest with a knife. After noticing the weapon, Shabana pushed them away and tried to flee while shouting for help. One of the colonists then stabbed him in the leg as he escaped. The man managed to continue running and contacted others for help, reporting that the colonists were pursuing him with knives. # Israel # Palestine # Gaza

"Je me suis promenée dans le quartier qui a été pris pour cible hier. C'était près de chez moi. Mais je n'ai pas pu prendre de photos du bâtiment touché. Une épaisse fumée s'en dégage encore, et les recherches pour retrouver les personnes qui se trouvaient à l'intérieur des appartements se poursuivent. Les corps semblent avoir été vaporisés par la force des missiles. Je n'ai pas jugé bon de sortir mon téléphone pour filmer des gens transportant les restes de leurs proches dans une situation aussi tragique." Nous sommes le samedi 16 mai 2026. La France est en week-end prolongé. Et c'est le message que je viens de recevoir d'une amie palestinienne à propos de l’attaque d'hier soir. Aujourd’hui, je ne trouve pas les mots pour lui répondre. Alors je publie ses propres mots. # Gaza # Palestine # StopGenocide # StopComplicesdeGenocides

Opinion. "Britain is losing the ability to tell anti-Semitism from dissent Pro-Palestinian protest is increasingly treated as inherently suspect. Jewish communities will not be made safer by the conflation, and democratic freedoms will not survive it. Sir Mark Rowley’s recent comments that some pro-Palestinian demonstrations in London send a message “that feels like anti-Semitism” are the latest sign of a dangerous trend in British public life: the conflation of anti-Semitism with criticism of the Israeli state. The Metropolitan Police commissioner suggested that some protest organisers deliberately route marches near synagogues in ways that intimidate British Jews. Any genuine intimidation of Jewish communities should, of course, be treated seriously. Anti-Semitism is real, dangerous and rising in Britain and across parts of Europe. It must be confronted clearly wherever it appears. But Britain is entering troubling territory when protests against the destruction of Gaza, opposition to Israeli state violence, or expressions of Palestinian grief are treated as inherently suspicious, even anti-Jewish, political acts." # uk # ukpol # antisemitism # zionism # palestine # gaza # Jews # Jewish https://www. aljazeera.com/opinions/2026/5/ 16/britain-is-losing-the-ability-to-tell-anti-semitism-from-dissent 🪡

Fatma Ubeyd, a 95-year-old Palestinian survivor of the 1948 Nakba, sits with children in a makeshift shelter. Her home in the Shuja’iyya neighbourhood was destroyed by recent Israeli military strikes. During the Nakba, which means ‘catastrophe’ in Arabic, about 700,000 Palestinians were expelled from their homeland after the creation of Israel. Photograph: Abdalhkem Abu Riash/Anadolu/Getty Images # photography # Gaza # Nakba # women # children

After committing a genocide in Gaza, and war crimes in Lebanon, Yemen, Iran, and Syria, is Netanyahu’s government collapsing ahead of elections? Benjamin Netanyahu’s ruling coalition submitted a proposal to dissolve the Knesset and hold early elections, signaling deepening political turmoil inside "Israel" as pressure mounts over the ongoing wars in Gaza, Lebanon, and Iran. The move comes amid growing anger from ultra-Orthodox parties who are saying that Netanyahu is failing to secure permanent military service exemptions for religious students, threatening the collapse of his coalition government. If approved, the legislation would trigger elections within 90 days, potentially bringing Israeli settlers to the polls by August as Netanyahu faces mounting criticism over the failure to achieve what he called “total victory” against Hamas, Hezbollah, and Iran after more than two years of regional war. Opposition leaders Yair Lapid and Naftali Bennett announced a joint electoral alliance aimed at challenging Netanyahu, while polls suggest the Israeli political scene remains deeply fragmented with no clear governing majority. # Israel # Palestine # Gaza # Genocide # Netanyahu # Syria # Hamas # Iran # Yemen # Lebanon # Hezbollah
